name: fsppo_longer_lr_cycle
hypothesis: Extending the cosine LR cycle from 50M to 100M steps will prevent learning rate from bottoming out too early during 200M step runs, allowing continued improvement in the second half of training.
rationale: Experiment #6 showed reward decline after 18M steps due to LR decay to 0. With 200M step runs and 50M cycles, the LR restarts 4 times but each restart can disrupt learned behavior. A single 100M cycle means 2 smoother warmups across the full run.
